scenario: A team has a deployed order-fulfillment v3. They discover that 50 in-flight instances are blocked because v3 has a defect in a downstream task. They want to migrate the 50 instances to v4 (the fix).
question: Which Operate feature handles this?

options.text: Cancelling and restarting each instance manually loses all in-flight state -- process variables, the current token position, any accumulated data from completed tasks. Restarting recreates fresh instances starting from the beginning, forcing all process logic to re-execute. This is disruptive to business operations and user experience. Process Instance Migration preserves instance continuity, migrating existing state to the new version without restarting the process from scratch.

A newly deployed v4 definition becomes the default for new instance starts, but existing v3 instances continue running on v3 until they complete, are cancelled, or are explicitly migrated via Process Instance Migration in Operate. Automatic migration would be disruptive -- it could move instances to positions in the new version that do not correspond to their current state.

Process Instance Migration in Operate allows active process instances running on an older process version to be moved to a newer deployed version without restarting them. A migration plan is created by mapping source flow-node IDs (v3) to target flow-node IDs (v4). Instances currently waiting at mapped source nodes are migrated to the corresponding target nodes, preserving instance state and variables. Operate applies the plan and the instances continue from their new mapped positions in the v4 definition.

Process Instance Migration is a supported feature in Camunda 8 Operate. It enables migration of active process instances between process definition versions by defining element-level mappings. This feature was introduced to support operational migrations when process improvements are deployed while instances are still in flight. Claiming migration is unsupported would be factually incorrect and would mislead operators into unnecessarily cancelling and restarting active business process instances. ###

a.text: Incorrect. Operate exposes Process Instance Migration: define mapping of source flow-nodes to target flow-nodes; apply to selected instances. Zeebe rebases the instances onto the target version preserving state where mappings exist.

c.text: Correct. No auto-migration; opt-in via migration feature.

explanation: No auto-migration; opt-in via migration feature.
